وَسَكَنتُمْ فِى مَسَـٰكِنِ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ وَتَبَيَّنَ لَكُمْ كَيْفَ فَعَلْنَا بِهِمْ وَضَرَبْنَا لَكُمُ ٱلْأَمْثَالَ

"And you lived in the dwellings of those who wronged themselves, and it became clear to you how We dealt with them, and We presented examples for you."

This ayah builds a careful argument. وَسَكَنتُمْ فِى مَسَـٰكِنِ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ - you lived in the very homes, in the very territories of people who wronged themselves and were destroyed. The word مَسَـٰكِنِ can mean literal dwellings or broader territories. You saw the ruins of past civilizations. You walked through the remnants of communities that were annihilated.

وَتَبَيَّنَ لَكُمْ كَيْفَ فَعَلْنَا بِهِمْ - and it became absolutely clear to you how We dealt with them. This wasn't hidden knowledge. The evidence was all around you.

وَضَرَبْنَا لَكُمُ ٱلْأَمْثَالَ - and We struck examples for you. Beyond the physical evidence, We gave you parables and stories in the Quran itself. You had every opportunity to learn.

translation: And they had planned their plan, but with Allah is [recorded] their plan, even if their plan had been [sufficient] to do away with the mountains
commentary:
وَقَدْ مَكَرُوا۟ مَكْرَهُمْ وَعِندَ ٱللَّهِ مَكْرُهُمْ وَإِن كَانَ مَكْرُهُمْ لِتَزُولَ مِنْهُ ٱلْجِبَالُ

"And they had planned their plan, but with Allah ﷻ is their plan, even if their plan was such that mountains would move from it."

مَكَرُوا۟ مَكْرَهُمْ - they plotted their plots. The essence of مَكْر is secret scheming. You don't share these plans with your enemy. Yet Allah ﷻ says وَعِندَ ٱللَّهِ مَكْرُهُمْ - with Allah is their plan. He possesses full access to their secret schemes.

The final phrase is grammatically rich: وَإِن كَانَ مَكْرُهُمْ لِتَزُولَ مِنْهُ ٱلْجِبَالُ. The particle إِن can mean "if" or can be used for negation. 

Read as negation: "And their plan was never going to move mountains" - their schemes were weak and ineffective.

Read as conditional: "Even if their plan was so elaborate that it would move mountains" - even at their strongest, Allah ﷻ still had complete control over their plots.

Both meanings convey the same ultimate message: whether their plans were weak or seemingly unstoppable, Allah ﷻ had them in His possession.

translation: So never think that Allah will fail in His promise to His messengers. Indeed, Allah is Exalted in Might and Owner of Retribution
commentary:
فَلَا تَحْسَبَنَّ ٱللَّهَ مُخْلِفَ وَعْدِهِۦ رُسُلَهُۥٓ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَزِيزٌ ذُو ٱنتِقَامٍ

"So never think that Allah ﷻ will fail in His promise to His messengers. Indeed, Allah ﷻ is Exalted in Might, the Owner of Retribution."

فَلَا تَحْسَبَنَّ - never, ever think. This is emphatic prohibition. Allah ﷻ will never go back on His promises to His messengers. The victory He promised will come.

إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَزِيزٌ ذُو ٱنتِقَامٍ - Indeed, Allah is Exalted in Might, Owner of Retribution. The combination of these two attributes is significant. عَزِيزٌ means He has ultimate authority and power. ذُو ٱنتِقَامٍ - Owner of Retribution.

There's a subtle difference between المُنْتَقِم (The Avenger) and ذُو ٱنتِقَامٍ (Owner of Retribution). By saying "Owner of Retribution" rather than simply "The Avenger," the focus is on capability and possession. Someone might take revenge but lack the power to do so effectively. But when you say He is the Owner of the very concept of retribution, it means His revenge is complete, total, and unstoppable.

translation: [It will be] on the Day the earth will be replaced by another earth, and the heavens [as well], and all creatures will come out before Allah, the One, the Prevailing
commentary:
يَوْمَ تُبَدَّلُ ٱلْأَرْضُ غَيْرَ ٱلْأَرْضِ وَٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتُ ۖ وَبَرَزُوا۟ لِلَّهِ ٱلْوَٰحِدِ ٱلْقَهَّارِ

"The Day the earth will be replaced with another earth, and the heavens as well, and they will come out before Allah ﷻ, the One, the Prevailing."

Notice something remarkable about this ayah. Throughout the Quran, when the heavens and earth are mentioned together, what's the typical order? السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ (the heavens and the earth) - heavens first, then earth. But on this Day, everything is reversed.

يَوْمَ تُبَدَّلُ ٱلْأَرْضُ غَيْرَ ٱلْأَرْضِ - the Day the earth will be changed into something other than the earth. People will look at it and say, "That's not the earth I knew." وَٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتُ - and the heavens too (though the rest is implied - they'll also be changed).

The very sequence of the ayah reflects its subject matter. Just as the earth and heavens will be fundamentally transformed, even the order in which they're mentioned is transformed.

وَبَرَزُوا۟ لِلَّهِ ٱلْوَٰحِدِ ٱلْقَهَّارِ - and they will come out, exposed, before Allah, the One, the Prevailing. الْقَهَّارِ describes the One you cannot escape no matter how hard you try. You can avoid, resist, hide all you want - you still cannot get away from Him.

translation: And you will see the criminals that Day bound together in shackles
commentary:
وَتَرَى ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ يَوْمَئِذٍ مُّقَرَّنِينَ فِى ٱلْأَصْفَادِ

"And you will see the criminals that Day bound together in shackles."

مُّقَرَّنِينَ comes from قَرَنَ, which means to tie two things together - like tying two camels together with the same rope. These criminals are shackled together, chained as one unit. فِى ٱلْأَصْفَادِ - in the shackles. الْأَصْفَادِ is the plural of صَفَد, which refers to metal shackles used on prisoners.

The imagery is of multiple criminals locked together in one continuous chain, unable to separate from each other, bound in their shared punishment.

translation: Their garments of liquid pitch and their faces covered by the Fire
commentary:
سَرَابِيلُهُم مِّن قَطِرَانٍ وَتَغْشَىٰ وُجُوهَهُمُ ٱلنَّارُ

"Their garments of liquid tar, and their faces covered by the Fire."

سَرَابِيلُهُم - their garments. سَرَابِيل is the plural of سِرْبَال, which refers to a complete outfit - shirt and pants, or a full jumpsuit. Interestingly, the original meaning of سِرْبَال in Arabic is clothing that protects from heat. In the harsh Arabian climate, heavy clothing actually protects you from the burning sun. But here, the concept is horrifically inverted.

مِّن قَطِرَانٍ - made of molten copper or liquid tar. The material of their "protective" clothing is molten metal, interacting with their skin, burning them continuously. As it burns their flesh, what emerges? Smoke.

وَتَغْشَىٰ وُجُوهَهُمُ ٱلنَّارُ - and the Fire covers their faces. The flames emanating from their burning garments rise up and engulf their faces. The smoke pours out, the flames consume them, and they remain chained to others experiencing the same torment.

This is Allah's ﷻ response to those who tortured the believers. The message to the suffering Muslims: don't worry, I've got them. What they did to you is nothing compared to what awaits them.

translation: So that Allah will recompense every soul for what it earned. Indeed, Allah is swift in account
commentary:
لِيَجْزِىَ ٱللَّهُ كُلَّ نَفْسٍ مَّا كَسَبَتْ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَرِيعُ ٱلْحِسَابِ

"So that Allah ﷻ may recompense every soul for what it earned. Indeed, Allah ﷻ is swift in account."

لِيَجْزِىَ ٱللَّهُ كُلَّ نَفْسٍ مَّا كَسَبَتْ - so that Allah may compensate every single person for exactly what he or she earned. Perfect justice. No one receives more or less than what they deserve.

إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَرِيعُ ٱلْحِسَابِ - Indeed, Allah is swift in reckoning. Don't think the delay means He's slow. When the accounting comes, it will be swift and complete.

translation: This [Qur'an] is notification for the people that they may be warned thereby and that they may know that He is but one God and that those of understanding will be reminded
commentary:
هَـٰذَا بَلَـٰغٌ لِّلنَّاسِ وَلِيُنذَرُوا۟ بِهِۦ وَلِيَعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّمَا هُوَ إِلَـٰهٌ وَٰحِدٌ وَلِيَذَّكَّرَ أُو۟لُوا۟ ٱلْأَلْبَـٰبِ

"This is notification for the people, that they may be warned by it, and that they may know that He is but one God, and that those of understanding will be reminded."

The surah concludes with this powerful declaration. هَـٰذَا بَلَـٰغٌ لِّلنَّاسِ - this is بَلَاغ for humanity. 

What is بَلَاغ? Literally, it means "to reach." But in rhetoric, it describes communication as a battle. You are the warrior, your words are your weapons, and the listener is your target. The real target isn't their ears - it's their heart. Your words must penetrate through all their mental defenses, all their barriers, all their reasons not to listen. When your words reach deep inside their heart and affect them, that's بَلَاغ - effective, penetrating, powerful communication.

The Quran describes itself as the ultimate بَلَاغ - the most direct, most effective, most penetrating speech.

وَلِيُنذَرُوا۟ بِهِۦ - so they may be warned by it. The first purpose: warning.

وَلِيَعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّمَا هُوَ إِلَـٰهٌ وَٰحِدٌ - and so they may come to know that He is only One God, single in His divinity. The second purpose: knowledge of Tawheed.

وَلِيَذَّكَّرَ أُو۟لُوا۟ ٱلْأَلْبَـٰابِ - and so that those of sound intellect, pure minds, may make the effort to remember. The third purpose: for people of deep understanding to reflect and be reminded.

This final ayah encapsulates the entire purpose of revelation: to warn, to teach Tawheed, and to awaken those capable of genuine reflection.
